ux_issues.description: Turn 1 initially returned 'no response from AI — please try again' error, requiring a retry after 15 seconds. Transient AI provider failure.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider auto-retry logic on the backend for transient AI provider failures before surfacing to the user.

ux_issues.description: AI called create_itinerary_items twice in Turns 1, 2, and 4 with identical payloads, resulting in the second call being rejected as 'already_exists'. This is wasteful and confusing.
ux_issues.suggestion: The AI should not re-call create_itinerary_items with the same items within the same turn. The dedup guard on the backend correctly prevents duplicates, but the AI wastes a tool loop iteration.

ux_issues.description: Day 9 item ordering is illogical: 'Island Exploration & Cultural Immersion' (order 1) comes before 'Arrival & Homestay Experience' (order 2) and 'Travel to Puno & Boat' (order 3). Should be travel first, then arrival, then exploration.
ux_issues.suggestion: AI should think about chronological ordering when generating itinerary items.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: All 6 planning turns executed correctly with zero fabrication bugs. create_itinerary_items fired reliably on every turn that required it (Turns 1-5) and correctly did NOT fire on the summary-only Turn 6. Itinerary grew monotonically: 16 -> 29 -> 33 -> 39 -> 46 items across turns, confirming no overwrite-instead-of-append bug. Turn 3 (food additions) correctly appended 4 new items to existing Cusco days without wiping them. Final itinerary covers all 10 days across Cusco (Days 1-4), Sacred Valley (Days 5-7), Machu Picchu (Day 8), and Lake Titicaca (Days 9-10) with 46 total items, well above the 15-item threshold. The only quality issues are duplicate tool calls within single turns and minor item ordering inconsistencies on Day 9. PR #198 fabrication detection is working correctly across multi-turn sessions.
